3. Has the respondent filed an acknowledgment of service? Note 3: The respondent can
file an acknowledgement of
Yes. A paper acknowledgement of service has been filed. service by sending a paper
Go to question 4. version to the court or by
completing it online using
Yes. An online acknowledgement of service has been filed. the MyHMCTS portal.
Go to Section C.
No. Go to question 6.
4. Who has signed the paper version of the acknowledgment of service?
the respondent (you must attach a copy of the
acknowledgment of service to this form, marked with an ‘A’)
a legal representative. Go to Section C.
5. I identify the signature appearing in the statement of truth signature
box on the paper version of the acknowledgement of service, a copy
of which I have attached to this statement and marked ‘A’, as the
signature of my
spouse
civil partner
who is the respondent in these proceedings.
Now go to Section C
6. The respondent has not filed an acknowledgement of service,
I have attached
a certificate of service (form FP6)
an order from the court dispensing with or deeming service
other evidence of service (specify below and attach any
relevant evidence)
Now go to Section C
Page 2
D84 Application for a conditional order or (judicial) separation order (03.24)

B. Joint applicants
This section should be completed by applicants who made the
divorce/dissolution/(judicial) separation application jointly. If you
wish to proceed as a sole applicant at this stage, please complete
question 10 below.
7. Full name of applicant 1
First name(s)
Middle name(s)
Last name
8. Full name of applicant 2
First name(s)
Middle name(s)
Last name
Complete either question 9 or question 10
9. This is a joint application and we both apply to the judge to make a
conditional order of
divorce in this case
dissolution in this case
or
in the case of (judicial) separation, the joint applicants apply to the
judge for a
(judicial) separation order in this case
Now go to Section C
Page 3
D84 Application for a conditional order or (judicial) separation order (03.24)

metadata.title: Apply for a conditional order or judicial separation order: Form D84
metadata.description: Use this form to apply for a conditional order confirming that the court does not see any reason why you cannot divorce or separate.
form_fields: Name of sole applicant or applicant 1
Or, in the case of (judicial) separation, the sole applicant applies to the judge for a (judicial) separation order in this case
